title: Psychotria boenyana
taxonomy: Psychotria boenyana belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf arrangement and flower structure. The genus Psychotria is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical regions and are often found in shaded environments. The Rubiaceae family is further divided into many subfamilies and tribes, with Psychotria being part of the tribe Psychotrieae. Within the family Rubiaceae, Psychotria boenyana is classified as a species that is endemic to Madagascar.

intro: Psychotria boenyana, also known as Boeny's psychotria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the island of Madagascar. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria boenyana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in folk medicine in Madagascar.
